NKJV.1: Song of Ascents. “Many a time they have afflicted me from my youth,” Let Israel now say—
NKJV.2: “Many a time they have afflicted me from my youth; Yet they have not prevailed against me.
NKJV.3: The plowers plowed on my back; They made their furrows long.”
NKJV.4: The LORD is righteous; He has cut in pieces the cords of the wicked.
NKJV.5: Let all those who hate Zion Be put to shame and turned back.
NKJV.6: Let them be as the grass on the housetops, Which withers before it grows up,
NKJV.7: With which the reaper does not fill his hand, Nor he who binds sheaves, his arms.
NKJV.8: Neither let those who pass by them say, “The blessing of the LORD be upon you; We bless you in the name of the LORD!”

KJV.1: A Song<S>7892</S> of degrees<S>4609</S>. Many a time<S>7227</S> have they afflicted<S>6887</S> me from my youth<S>5271</S>, may Israel<S>3478</S> now say<S>559</S>: <sup>Many: or, Much</sup>
KJV.2: Many a time<S>7227</S> have they afflicted<S>6887</S> me from my youth<S>5271</S>: yet they have not prevailed<S>3201</S> against me. <sup>Many: or, Much</sup>
KJV.3: The plowers<S>2790</S> plowed<S>2790</S> upon my back<S>1354</S>: they made long<S>748</S> their furrows<S>4618</S> <S>4618</S>.

ESV.1: “Greatly  have they afflicted me from my youth” — let Israel now say —
ESV.2: “Greatly have they afflicted me from my youth, yet they have not prevailed against me.
ESV.3: The plowers plowed upon my back; they made long their furrows.”
ESV.4: The LORD is righteous; he has cut the cords of the wicked.
ESV.5: May all who hate Zion be put to shame and turned backward!
ESV.6: Let them be like the grass on the housetops, which withers before it grows up,
ESV.7: with which the reaper does not fill his hand nor the binder of sheaves his arms,
ESV.8: nor do those who pass by say, “The blessing of the LORD be upon you! We bless you in the name of the LORD!”

NASB.1: A Song of Ascents. 'Many times they have persecuted me from my youth up,' Let Israel now say,
NASB.2: 'Many times they have persecuted me from my youth up; Yet they have not prevailed against me.
NASB.3: 'The plowers plowed upon my back; They lengthened their furrows.'
NASB.4: The LORD is righteous; He has cut in two the cords of the wicked.
NASB.5: May all who hate Zion Be put to shame and turned backward;
NASB.6: Let them be like grass upon the housetops, Which withers before it grows up;
NASB.7: With which the reaper does not fill his hand, Or the binder of sheaves his bosom;
NASB.8: Nor do those who pass by say, 'The blessing of the LORD be upon you; We bless you in the name of the LORD.'

NLT.1:
From my earliest youth my enemies have persecuted me.
Let all Israel repeat this:
NLT.2:
From my earliest youth my enemies have persecuted me,
but they have never defeated me.
NLT.3:
My back is covered with cuts,
as if a farmer had plowed long furrows.
NLT.4:
But the LORD is good;
he has cut me free from the ropes of the ungodly.
NLT.5:
May all who hate Jerusalem
be turned back in shameful defeat.
NLT.6:
May they be as useless as grass on a rooftop,
turning yellow when only half grown,
NLT.7:
ignored by the harvester,
despised by the binder.
NLT.8:
And may those who pass by
refuse to give them this blessing:
“The LORD bless you;
we bless you in the LORD’s name.”

WEB.1: A Song of Ascents. Many times they have afflicted me from my youth up. Let Israel now say,
WEB.2: many times they have afflicted me from my youth up, yet they have not prevailed against me.
WEB.3: The plowers plowed on my back. They made their furrows long.
WEB.4: Yahweh is righteous. He has cut apart the cords of the wicked.
WEB.5: Let them be disappointed and turned backward, all those who hate Zion.
WEB.6: Let them be as the grass on the housetops, which withers before it grows up;
WEB.7: with which the reaper doesn’t fill his hand, nor he who binds sheaves, his bosom.
WEB.8: Neither do those who go by say, “The blessing of Yahweh be on you. We bless you in Yahweh’s name.”

CSB17.1:
A song of ascents. [1] Since my youth they have often attacked me —
let Israel say —
CSB17.2:
Since my youth they have often attacked me,
but they have not prevailed against me. ⓔ
CSB17.3:
Plowmen plowed over my back;
they made their furrows long. ⓕ
CSB17.4:
The LORD is righteous;
he has cut the ropes of the wicked. ⓖ
CSB17.5:
Let all who hate Zion
be driven back in disgrace. ⓗ
CSB17.6:
Let them be like grass on the rooftops,
which withers before it grows up [1] ⓘ
CSB17.7:
and can’t even fill the hands of the reaper
or the arms of the one who binds sheaves. ⓙ
CSB17.8:
Then none who pass by will say,
“May the LORD’s blessing be on you.
We bless you in the name of the LORD.” ⓚ

AMP.1:
A Song of Ascents. "Many times they have persecuted me (Israel) from my youth,"
Let Israel now say,
AMP.2:
"Many times they have persecuted me from my youth,
Yet they have not prevailed against me.
AMP.3:
"The [enemies, like] plowers plowed on my back;
They made their furrows [of suffering] long [in Israel]."
AMP.4:
The LORD is righteous;
He has cut in two the [thick] cords of the wicked [which enslaved the people of Israel].
AMP.5:
May all who hate Zion
Be put to shame and turned backward [in defeat].
AMP.6:
Let them be like the grass on the housetops,
Which withers before it grows up,
AMP.7:
With which the reaper does not fill his hand,
Nor the binder of sheaves his arms,
AMP.8:
Nor do those who pass by say,
"The blessing of the LORD be upon you;
We bless you in the name of the LORD."

MSG.1:
A pilgrim song
"They've kicked me around ever since I was young"
—this is how Israel tells it—
MSG.2:
"They've kicked me around ever since I was young,
but they never could keep me down.
MSG.3:
Their plowmen plowed long furrows
up and down my back;
MSG.4:
Then God ripped the harnesses
of the evil plowmen to shreds."
MSG.5:
Oh, let all those who hate Zion
grovel in humiliation;
MSG.6:
Let them be like grass in shallow ground
that withers before the harvest,
MSG.7:
Before the farmhands can gather it in,
the harvesters get in the crop,
MSG.8:
Before the neighbors have a chance to call out,
"Congratulations on your wonderful crop!
We bless you in God's name!"

NIV.1:
Psalm 129
A song of ascents.
They have greatly oppressed me from my youth—
let Israel say—
NIV.2:
they have greatly oppressed me from my youth,
but they have not gained the victory over me.
NIV.3:
Plowmen have plowed my back
and made their furrows long.
NIV.4:
But the Lord is righteous;
he has cut me free from the cords of the wicked.
NIV.5:
May all who hate Zion
be turned back in shame.
NIV.6:
May they be like grass on the roof,
which withers before it can grow;
NIV.7:
with it the reaper cannot fill his hands,
nor the one who gathers fill his arms.
NIV.8:
May those who pass by not say,
“The blessing of the Lord be upon you;
we bless you in the name of the Lord.”

NRSVCE.1:
“Often have they attacked me from my youth”
— let Israel now say —
NRSVCE.2:
“often have they attacked me from my youth,
yet they have not prevailed against me.
NRSVCE.3:
The plowers plowed on my back;
they made their furrows long.”
NRSVCE.4:
The LORD is righteous;
he has cut the cords of the wicked.
NRSVCE.5:
May all who hate Zion
be put to shame and turned backward.
NRSVCE.6:
Let them be like the grass on the housetops
that withers before it grows up,
NRSVCE.7:
with which reapers do not fill their hands
or binders of sheaves their arms,
NRSVCE.8:
while those who pass by do not say,
“The blessing of the LORD be upon you!
We bless you in the name of the LORD!”

NET.1: “Since my youth they have often attacked me,” let Israel say.
NET.2: “Since my youth they have often attacked me, but they have not defeated me.
NET.3: The plowers plowed my back; they made their furrows long.
NET.4: The LORD is just; he cut the ropes of the wicked.”
NET.5: May all who hate Zion be humiliated and turned back!
NET.6: May they be like the grass on the rooftops which withers before one can even pull it up,
NET.7: which cannot fill the reaper’s hand, or the lap of the one who gathers the grain!
NET.8: Those who pass by will not say, “May you experience the LORD’s blessing! We pronounce a blessing on you in the name of the LORD.”
